briefdescription.para: Flag indicating a work item that is running under a work queue thread.

detaileddescription.para: Accessed via k_work_busy_get(). May co-occur with other flags.

briefdescription.para: Flag indicating a work item that is being canceled.

para.verbatim: Accessed via k_work_busy_get(). May co-occur with other flags.

briefdescription.para: Flag indicating a work item that has been submitted to a queue but has not started running.

detaileddescription.para: Accessed via k_work_busy_get(). May co-occur with other flags.

briefdescription.para: Flag indicating a delayed work item that is scheduled for submission to a queue.

detaileddescription.para: Accessed via k_work_busy_get(). May co-occur with other flags.

briefdescription.para: Flag indicating a synced work item that is being flushed.

para.verbatim: Accessed via k_work_busy_get(). May co-occur with other flags.

briefdescription.para: The signature for a work item handler function.

detaileddescription.para: The function will be invoked by the thread animating a work queue.

parameterdescription.para: the work item that provided the handler.

briefdescription.para: Work item handler function type for user work queues.

detaileddescription.para: A work item's handler function is executed by a user workqueue's thread when the work item is processed by the workqueue.

parameterdescription.para: Address of the work item.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a (non-delayable) work structure.

detaileddescription.para: This must be invoked before submitting a work structure for the first time. It need not be invoked again on the same work structure. It can be re-invoked to change the associated handler, but this must be done when the work item is idle.

parameterdescription.para: the work structure to be initialized.
the handler to be invoked by the work item.

briefdescription.para: Busy state flags from the work item.

detaileddescription.para: A zero return value indicates the work item appears to be idle.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result is checked. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

simplesect.para: a mask of flags K_WORK_DELAYED, K_WORK_QUEUED, K_WORK_RUNNING, K_WORK_CANCELING, and K_WORK_FLUSHING.

briefdescription.para: Test whether a work item is currently pending.

detaileddescription.para: Wrapper to determine whether a work item is in a non-idle state.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result is checked. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

simplesect.para: true if and only if k_work_busy_get() returns a non-zero value.

briefdescription.para: Submit a work item to a queue.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work queue on which the item should run. If NULL the queue from the most recent submission will be used.
pointer to the work item.
if work was already submitted to a queue
if work was not submitted and has been queued to queue
if work was running and has been queued to the queue that was running it

listitem.para: if work submission was rejected because the work item is cancelling; or

parameterdescription.para: if queue is null and the work item has never been run.
if queue has not been started.

briefdescription.para: Submit a work item to the system queue.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

simplesect.para: as with k_work_submit_to_queue().

briefdescription.para: Wait for last-submitted instance to complete.

detaileddescription.para: Resubmissions may occur while waiting, including chained submissions (from within the handler).

simplesect.para: Be careful of caller and work queue thread relative priority. If this function sleeps it will not return until the work queue thread completes the tasks that allow this thread to resume.
Behavior is undefined if this function is invoked on work from a work queue running work.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

pointer to an opaque item containing state related to the pending cancellation. The object must persist until the call returns, and be accessible from both the caller thread and the work queue thread. The object must not be used for any other flush or cancel operation until this one completes. On architectures with CONFIG_KERNEL_COHERENCE the object must be allocated in coherent memory.
if call had to wait for completion
if work was already idle

briefdescription.para: Cancel a work item.

detaileddescription.para: This attempts to prevent a pending (non-delayable) work item from being processed by removing it from the work queue. If the item is being processed, the work item will continue to be processed, but resubmissions are rejected until cancellation completes.
If this returns zero cancellation is complete, otherwise something (probably a work queue thread) is still referencing the item.
See also k_work_cancel_sync().

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

simplesect.para: the k_work_busy_get() status indicating the state of the item after all cancellation steps performed by this call are completed.

briefdescription.para: Cancel a work item and wait for it to complete.

detaileddescription.para: Same as k_work_cancel() but does not return until cancellation is complete. This can be invoked by a thread after k_work_cancel() to synchronize with a previous cancellation.
On return the work structure will be idle unless something submits it after the cancellation was complete.

simplesect.para: Be careful of caller and work queue thread relative priority. If this function sleeps it will not return until the work queue thread completes the tasks that allow this thread to resume.
Behavior is undefined if this function is invoked on work from a work queue running work.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the work item.

pointer to an opaque item containing state related to the pending cancellation. The object must persist until the call returns, and be accessible from both the caller thread and the work queue thread. The object must not be used for any other flush or cancel operation until this one completes. On architectures with CONFIG_KERNEL_COHERENCE the object must be allocated in coherent memory.
if work was pending (call had to wait for cancellation of a running handler to complete, or scheduled or submitted operations were cancelled);
otherwise

briefdescription.para: Initialize a work queue structure.

detaileddescription.para: This must be invoked before starting a work queue structure for the first time. It need not be invoked again on the same work queue structure.

parameterdescription.para: the queue structure to be initialized.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_work_q *queue, k_thread_stack_t *stack, size_t stack_size, int prio, const struct k_work_queue_config *cfg)
memberdef.name: k_work_queue_start

briefdescription.para: Initialize a work queue.

detaileddescription.para: This configures the work queue thread and starts it running. The function should not be re-invoked on a queue.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the queue structure. It must be initialized in zeroed/bss memory or with k_work_queue_init before use.
pointer to the work thread stack area.
size of the work thread stack area, in bytes.
initial thread priority
optional additional configuration parameters. Pass NULL if not required, to use the defaults documented in k_work_queue_config.

briefdescription.para: Run work queue using calling thread.

detaileddescription.para: This will run the work queue forever unless stopped by k_work_queue_stop.

parameterdescription.para: the queue to run
optional additional configuration parameters. Pass NULL if not required, to use the defaults documented in k_work_queue_config.

briefdescription.para: Access the thread that animates a work queue.

detaileddescription.para: This is necessary to grant a work queue thread access to things the work items it will process are expected to use.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the queue structure.

simplesect.para: the thread associated with the work queue.

briefdescription.para: Wait until the work queue has drained, optionally plugging it.

detaileddescription.para: This blocks submission to the work queue except when coming from queue thread, and blocks the caller until no more work items are available in the queue.
If plug is true then submission will continue to be blocked after the drain operation completes until k_work_queue_unplug() is invoked.

Note that work items that are delayed are not yet associated with their work queue. They must be cancelled externally if a goal is to ensure the work queue remains empty. The plug feature can be used to prevent delayed items from being submitted after the drain completes.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the queue structure.
if true the work queue will continue to block new submissions after all items have drained.
if call had to wait for the drain to complete
if call did not have to wait
if wait was interrupted or failed

briefdescription.para: Release a work queue to accept new submissions.

detaileddescription.para: This releases the block on new submissions placed when k_work_queue_drain() is invoked with the plug option enabled. If this is invoked before the drain completes new items may be submitted as soon as the drain completes.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the queue structure.
if successfully unplugged
if the work queue was not plugged.

briefdescription.para: Stop a work queue.

detaileddescription.para: Stops the work queue thread and ensures that no further work will be processed. This call is blocking and guarantees that the work queue thread has terminated cleanly if successful, no work will be processed past this point.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue structure.
Maximum time to wait for the work queue to stop.
if the work queue was stopped
if the work queue was not started (or already stopped)
if the work queue is actively processing work items
if the work queue did not stop within the stipulated timeout
if the work queue is essential

briefdescription.para: Initialize a delayable work structure.

detaileddescription.para: This must be invoked before scheduling a delayable work structure for the first time. It need not be invoked again on the same work structure. It can be re-invoked to change the associated handler, but this must be done when the work item is idle.

parameterdescription.para: the delayable work structure to be initialized.
the handler to be invoked by the work item.

briefdescription.para: Get the parent delayable work structure from a work pointer.

detaileddescription.para: This function is necessary when a k_work_handler_t function is passed to k_work_schedule_for_queue() and the handler needs to access data from the container of the containing k_work_delayable.

parameterdescription.para: Address passed to the work handler

simplesect.para: Address of the containing k_work_delayable structure.

briefdescription.para: Busy state flags from the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result can be inspected. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: a mask of flags K_WORK_DELAYED, K_WORK_QUEUED, K_WORK_RUNNING, K_WORK_CANCELING, and K_WORK_FLUSHING. A zero return value indicates the work item appears to be idle.

briefdescription.para: Test whether a delayed work item is currently pending.

detaileddescription.para: Wrapper to determine whether a delayed work item is in a non-idle state.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result can be inspected. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: true if and only if k_work_delayable_busy_get() returns a non-zero value.

briefdescription.para: Get the absolute tick count at which a scheduled delayable work will be submitted.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result can be inspected. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: the tick count when the timer that will schedule the work item will expire, or the current tick count if the work is not scheduled.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of ticks until a scheduled delayable work will be submitted.

simplesect.para: This is a live snapshot of state, which may change before the result can be inspected. Use locks where appropriate.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: the number of ticks until the timer that will schedule the work item will expire, or zero if the item is not scheduled.

briefdescription.para: Submit an idle work item to a queue after a delay.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike k_work_reschedule_for_queue() this is a no-op if the work item is already scheduled or submitted, even if delay is K_NO_WAIT.

parameterdescription.para: the queue on which the work item should be submitted after the delay.
pointer to the delayable work item.
the time to wait before submitting the work item. If K_NO_WAIT and the work is not pending this is equivalent to k_work_submit_to_queue().
if work was already scheduled or submitted.
if work has been scheduled.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and work was running and has been queued to the queue that was running it.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.

briefdescription.para: Submit an idle work item to the system work queue after a delay.

detaileddescription.para: This is a thin wrapper around k_work_schedule_for_queue(), with all the API characteristics of that function.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.
the time to wait before submitting the work item. If K_NO_WAIT this is equivalent to k_work_submit_to_queue().

simplesect.para: as with k_work_schedule_for_queue().

briefdescription.para: Reschedule a work item to a queue after a delay.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike k_work_schedule_for_queue() this function can change the deadline of a scheduled work item, and will schedule a work item that is in any state (e.g. is idle, submitted, or running). This function does not affect ("unsubmit") a work item that has been submitted to a queue.

parameterdescription.para: the queue on which the work item should be submitted after the delay.
pointer to the delayable work item.
the time to wait before submitting the work item. If K_NO_WAIT this is equivalent to k_work_submit_to_queue() after canceling any previous scheduled submission.

simplesect.para: If delay is K_NO_WAIT ("no delay") the return values are as with k_work_submit_to_queue().

parameterdescription.para: if delay is K_NO_WAIT and work was already on a queue

if
delay is K_NO_WAIT and work was not submitted but has now been queued to queue; or
delay not K_NO_WAIT and work has been scheduled
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and work was running and has been queued to the queue that was running it
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.
if delay is K_NO_WAIT and k_work_submit_to_queue() fails with this code.

briefdescription.para: Reschedule a work item to the system work queue after a delay.

detaileddescription.para: This is a thin wrapper around k_work_reschedule_for_queue(), with all the API characteristics of that function.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.
the time to wait before submitting the work item.

simplesect.para: as with k_work_reschedule_for_queue().

briefdescription.para: Flush delayable work.

detaileddescription.para: If the work is scheduled, it is immediately submitted. Then the caller blocks until the work completes, as with k_work_flush().

simplesect.para: Be careful of caller and work queue thread relative priority. If this function sleeps it will not return until the work queue thread completes the tasks that allow this thread to resume.
Behavior is undefined if this function is invoked on dwork from a work queue running dwork.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

pointer to an opaque item containing state related to the pending cancellation. The object must persist until the call returns, and be accessible from both the caller thread and the work queue thread. The object must not be used for any other flush or cancel operation until this one completes. On architectures with CONFIG_KERNEL_COHERENCE the object must be allocated in coherent memory.
if call had to wait for completion
if work was already idle

briefdescription.para: Cancel delayable work.

detaileddescription.para: Similar to k_work_cancel() but for delayable work. If the work is scheduled or submitted it is canceled. This function does not wait for the cancellation to complete.

simplesect.para: The work may still be running when this returns. Use k_work_flush_delayable() or k_work_cancel_delayable_sync() to ensure it is not running.
Canceling delayable work does not prevent rescheduling it. It does prevent submitting it until the cancellation completes.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

simplesect.para: the k_work_delayable_busy_get() status indicating the state of the item after all cancellation steps performed by this call are completed.

briefdescription.para: Cancel delayable work and wait.

detaileddescription.para: Like k_work_cancel_delayable() but waits until the work becomes idle.

simplesect.para: Canceling delayable work does not prevent rescheduling it. It does prevent submitting it until the cancellation completes.
Be careful of caller and work queue thread relative priority. If this function sleeps it will not return until the work queue thread completes the tasks that allow this thread to resume.
Behavior is undefined if this function is invoked on dwork from a work queue running dwork.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the delayable work item.

pointer to an opaque item containing state related to the pending cancellation. The object must persist until the call returns, and be accessible from both the caller thread and the work queue thread. The object must not be used for any other flush or cancel operation until this one completes. On architectures with CONFIG_KERNEL_COHERENCE the object must be allocated in coherent memory.
if work was not idle (call had to wait for cancellation of a running handler to complete, or scheduled or submitted operations were cancelled);
otherwise

briefdescription.para: Initialize a userspace work item.

detaileddescription.para: This routine initializes a user workqueue work item, prior to its first use.

parameterdescription.para: Address of work item.
Function to invoke each time work item is processed.

briefdescription.para: Check if a userspace work item is pending.

detaileddescription.para: This routine indicates if user work item work is pending in a workqueue's queue.

simplesect.para: Checking if the work is pending gives no guarantee that the work will still be pending when this information is used. It is up to the caller to make sure that this information is used in a safe manner.

parameterdescription.para: Address of work item.

simplesect.para: true if work item is pending, or false if it is not pending.

briefdescription.para: Submit a work item to a user mode workqueue.

detaileddescription.para: Submits a work item to a workqueue that runs in user mode. A temporary memory allocation is made from the caller's resource pool which is freed once the worker thread consumes the k_work item. The workqueue thread must have memory access to the k_work item being submitted. The caller must have permission granted on the work_q parameter's queue object.

parameterdescription.para: Address of workqueue.
Address of work item.
if the work item was already in some workqueue
if no memory for thread resource pool allocation
Success

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_work_user_q *work_q, k_thread_stack_t *stack, size_t stack_size, int prio, const char *name)
memberdef.name: k_work_user_queue_start

briefdescription.para: Start a workqueue in user mode.

detaileddescription.para: This works identically to k_work_queue_start() except it is callable from user mode, and the worker thread created will run in user mode. The caller must have permissions granted on both the work_q parameter's thread and queue objects, and the same restrictions on priority apply as k_thread_create().

parameterdescription.para: Address of workqueue.
Pointer to work queue thread's stack space, as defined by K_THREAD_STACK_DEFINE()
Size of the work queue thread's stack (in bytes), which should either be the same constant passed to K_THREAD_STACK_DEFINE() or the value of K_THREAD_STACK_SIZEOF().
Priority of the work queue's thread.
optional thread name. If not null a copy is made into the thread's name buffer.

briefdescription.para: Access the user mode thread that animates a work queue.

detaileddescription.para: This is necessary to grant a user mode work queue thread access to things the work items it will process are expected to use.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the user mode queue structure.

simplesect.para: the user mode thread associated with the work queue.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a triggered work item.

detaileddescription.para: This routine initializes a workqueue triggered work item, prior to its first use.

parameterdescription.para: Address of triggered work item.
Function to invoke each time work item is processed.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_work_q *work_q, struct k_work_poll *work, struct k_poll_event *events, int num_events, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: k_work_poll_submit_to_queue

briefdescription.para: Submit a triggered work item.

detaileddescription.para: This routine schedules work item work to be processed by workqueue work_q when one of the given events is signaled. The routine initiates internal poller for the work item and then returns to the caller. Only when one of the watched events happen the work item is actually submitted to the workqueue and becomes pending.

Submitting a previously submitted triggered work item that is still waiting for the event cancels the existing submission and reschedules it the using the new event list. Note that this behavior is inherently subject to race conditions with the pre-existing triggered work item and work queue, so care must be taken to synchronize such resubmissions externally.

simplesect.para: Provided array of events as well as a triggered work item must be placed in persistent memory (valid until work handler execution or work cancellation) and cannot be modified after submission.

parameterdescription.para: Address of workqueue.
Address of delayed work item.
An array of events which trigger the work.
The number of events in the array.
Timeout after which the work will be scheduled for execution even if not triggered.
Work item started watching for events.
Work item is being processed or has completed its work.
Work item is pending on a different workqueue.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_work_poll *work, struct k_poll_event *events, int num_events, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: k_work_poll_submit

briefdescription.para: Submit a triggered work item to the system workqueue.

detaileddescription.para: This routine schedules work item work to be processed by system workqueue when one of the given events is signaled. The routine initiates internal poller for the work item and then returns to the caller. Only when one of the watched events happen the work item is actually submitted to the workqueue and becomes pending.

Submitting a previously submitted triggered work item that is still waiting for the event cancels the existing submission and reschedules it the using the new event list. Note that this behavior is inherently subject to race conditions with the pre-existing triggered work item and work queue, so care must be taken to synchronize such resubmissions externally.

simplesect.para: Provided array of events as well as a triggered work item must not be modified until the item has been processed by the workqueue.

parameterdescription.para: Address of delayed work item.
An array of events which trigger the work.
The number of events in the array.
Timeout after which the work will be scheduled for execution even if not triggered.
Work item started watching for events.
Work item is being processed or has completed its work.
Work item is pending on a different workqueue.

briefdescription.para: Cancel a triggered work item.

detaileddescription.para: This routine cancels the submission of triggered work item work. A triggered work item can only be canceled if no event triggered work submission.

parameterdescription.para: Address of delayed work item.
Work item canceled.
Work item is being processed or has completed its work.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a statically-defined delayable work item.

detaileddescription.para: This macro can be used to initialize a statically-defined delayable work item, prior to its first use. For example,

Note that if the runtime dependencies support initialization with k_work_init_delayable() using that will eliminate the initialized object in ROM that is produced by this macro and copied in at system startup.

parameterdescription.para: Symbol name for delayable work item object
Function to invoke each time work item is processed.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a statically-defined user work item.

detaileddescription.para: This macro can be used to initialize a statically-defined user work item, prior to its first use. For example,

parameterdescription.para: Symbol name for work item object
Function to invoke each time work item is processed.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a statically-defined work item.

detaileddescription.para: This macro can be used to initialize a statically-defined workqueue work item, prior to its first use. For example,

parameterdescription.para: Symbol name for work item object
Function to invoke each time work item is processed.
